article.title: 100+ interviews | 50+ companies | 0 offers
article.content:
As the title says, gave 100+ interviews over a period of 1 year. The only constant in my journey is failure. 

Gave up and thinking to leave the software. Let me know your thoughts. 

YOE: 3
I Can share detailed interview experience for any companies if required.

Edit: 

Thanks for the valuable comments. I know my preparation was not sufficient in the first half. But I feel it was good enough in the 2nd half.

---


Preparation strategy:
DSA: Was consistent. LC stats: 1000+ problems only 100 hard (Count doesn't matter, even 300 are good enough) 

System design: Prepared with Alex xu book, Shrayansh jain and Gaurav sen's youtube, Ashish pratap singh's github repo. 


---

Still I'm weaker.

In DSA, I'm getting failed if they ask any LC questions which I've came across and having Less acceptance rate. 

In system design, I've no clue. Will try to give mock. Any other Suggestions are welcomed for preparation.

---

post.content: I think, you need to analayse the faiure instead of just count the failure. \nThe big thing is that you are getting the call in this down period. If you think you can connect to me also.
No offense but I think you need to prep more/better. This is a lot of interviews, kind of a waste that you weren\'t prepared enough.

I hope you read this. I had a similar sheet and first it was DSA, then LLD, then HLD which I had to fix in a span of 7 months. 
 I later did get 2 offers from big tech and the problem always had been behavioral(not for google off course, they dont care much for that). 1000 leecode, 1200 is insignificant, if you have gives many I guess you already know most of the questions are on same 250 odd question list and some variation, nothing else. No one is there to test you with a brain teaser which even they wont be able to do on a bad day.  Coding interviews are just filters if you can code or not or maybe you put in the work to join this company or not. 

All the roles you are applying are senior roles, No where in your post I saw you acknowledging behavioral or if you worked on it, especially SDE 2, your stories cant be "build a dashboard", "fixed production defect", did this, and that, the stories have to be impactful. All these HLD design questions make zero sense if the knowledge you are sharing in those system design is actually believable, if your stories are not based on the work or impact you had done. 
If its META which pretty much starts with no intro that is purely for design, but with amazon, microsoft, salesforce, linkedin even they will ask and probe you for behavioral. 

Reach out to a manager or someone senior or someone, mock those stories, record yourself live interview, check where you fault was. The story should come naturally. Try to do mocks, pay some extra and get that feedback. 
Good luck.
just learn from failures and keep moving.. it happens sometimes, don\'t worry & good luck bro\u2764

It looks like interviewing more companies won\'t help since you won\'t get valuable feedback on why you fail. Do some mock interviews on meetapro  and see what you need to improve. After that, better spend more time on preparation to address the feedback.

I\'ve heard people get many interviews if they lie pretending that they have FAANG experience. If you get that many interviews and get rejected every time, maybe there are something false in your resume. If not, maybe there are some other red flags.\nI also get rejected again and again, but I don\'t even get interviewed by good companies like those in your list.\nI think if I manage to get a job at this difficult times, I will be bragging about it forever.

You have solved 1000+ problems and given 50+ interviews. I think you would have gotten problems you have solved or very-very similar to ones you have solved. Were you able to solve those in interviews??

How did u land so many interviews? Im trying for an year and I have my fourth interview scheduled which I know would be a failure. In my opinion u have learnt more than those who have not given many interviews
hi bro need some help can we connect
always speed matters in interviews which is why i failed recently in few interviews. repeated practice might help ...
What is your current company, YOE and CTC?

Hey, thanks for sharing your journey, respect for the persistence. As a fresher with just a few months of experience, even getting my resume shortlisted feels like a big hurdle. Could you share some highlights of your resume (what you emphasized, how you structured it)? It might help folks who are early in their careers.
It might look like selfish on my part, but is it possible for you to share interview experience/questions of Persistent Systems, I have my interview on upcoming Saturday.

Focus on mocks. I can see that you\'ve got persistence, but you might be struggling with performance on the day of the interview. Try to simulate real interview-like scenarios with your friends or use https://mockingly.ai for this.\n\nBut your problem may not be just technical skills. It might be interview anxiety. We all had it, don\'t worry!
